meta.certificationName: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer
meta.title: GCP-MLE-6: Vertex AI Production ML, Pipelines, Monitoring, and Responsible AI

domains.name: Monitoring, Optimizing, and Maintaining ML Solutions

questions.stem: A project team needs to make a certification-aligned design decision about framing an ML problem with measurable business and model objectives. What is the BEST choice?

options.text: Use the managed capability or documented pattern for framing an ML problem with measurable business and model objectives, then validate it with realistic workload or evaluation data
Choose the largest model or compute shape for every workload and ignore latency, governance, and cost constraints
Move the decision entirely to end users at runtime without documenting defaults, limits, or fallback behavior
Treat the feature as complete after a local prototype, without testing service integration or operational behavior

questions.explanation: A is correct because the supporting documentation describes framing an ML problem with measurable business and model objectives as an explicit design or operational concern that should be configured and validated. B is incorrect because blindly maximizing resources ignores fit-for-purpose architecture. C is incorrect because runtime ambiguity creates inconsistent and hard-to-govern behavior. D is incorrect because a prototype alone does not prove production readiness. Reference: [Vertex AI custom training overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview)

questions.stem: A team is designing a solution involving choosing custom training when prebuilt options do not fit requirements. Which TWO actions best align with recommended practice for this objective? (Select TWO)

options.text: Apply choosing custom training when prebuilt options do not fit requirements deliberately and verify the behavior with representative test cases
Rely on manual, undocumented steps because managed AI and ML services infer the intended production behavior automatically
Monitor the outcome of choosing custom training when prebuilt options do not fit requirements with service metrics, logs, evaluations, or review evidence appropriate to the risk
Bypass access controls and validation temporarily so the workflow can collect more context from every available source

questions.explanation: A and C are correct because the referenced documentation supports intentional configuration of choosing custom training when prebuilt options do not fit requirements and operational verification. B is incorrect because production AI and ML systems require explicit, repeatable design rather than undocumented manual behavior. D is incorrect because broad bypass of controls conflicts with secure and governed implementation. Reference: [Vertex AI custom training overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview)
